reasoning: HKU5-CoV-2 is a newly characterized bat coronavirus that received media attention in late February 2025 after a study showed it could use the human ACE2 receptor to enter cells in a laboratory setting. However, as of mid-May 2025, there are absolutely no reported human cases of this virus. The CDC and global health experts have explicitly stated it is not currently a public health threat and that there is no evidence it can easily infect humans in real-world conditions (in fact, it enters human cells significantly less efficiently than SARS-CoV-2). Given that we are predicting a spillover event of this one specific animal virus within a brief 1.5-month window (by July 1, 2025), the base rate is astronomically low. The probability is set to the lowest allowed bound of 5%.
